<findings>

[observation] [warning] 10 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
[observation] [info] Bidirectional imports with web-application-core (38 total) suggest the community detection threshold fragmented what is architecturally a single layered zone; consolidating messaging and peripheral components into the core zone may better reflect intent.
[observation] [info] MCP route handler (routes-mcp.ts) landing in this peripheral zone rather than the main server cluster may make it harder to audit the full MCP request lifecycle alongside the other server routes in web-application-core.
[observation] [info] Static binary assets (PNG files) and build scripts are co-clustered with runtime messaging modules; these artifacts have no import relationships and are included purely by package-level proximity, not semantic cohesion.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "web-viewer-messaging-pipeline" has files across 11 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [warning] This is the only web zone meeting the joint fragility criterion (cohesion < 0.6 AND coupling > 0.3). Its 10 entry points expose a wide API surface into bidirectionally-coupled neighbors. Treat refactoring changes in this zone as high-risk until cohesion improves or the catch-all content is redistributed.
[suggestion] [info] Zone name 'messaging-pipeline' is misleading: it groups server route handlers, build scripts, static image assets, peripheral UI components, and messaging infrastructure under a single messaging-specific label. Rename to 'web-peripheral' or 'web-seam' to reflect its catch-all reality, or split into a dedicated messaging zone and a build-artifacts zone to bring the name into alignment with actual content.
[relationship] [warning] routes-mcp.ts has exactly one consumer (server/start.ts) inside the server composition root but landed in the messaging-pipeline zone; this placement splits the MCP request lifecycle across two zones and obscures the full server routing surface during audits.
[anti-pattern] [info] performance/index.ts re-exports all of crash-detector's public symbols from ../crash/index.js, creating a second canonical path for the same module. Consumers that import crash symbols from performance/index.ts will not appear as direct crash-zone dependents in import graph analysis, making crash zone coupling invisible to tooling.

</findings>

<insights>

- 10 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- Bidirectional imports with web-application-core (22 inbound + 16 outbound = 38 total) suggest the community algorithm split a single logical cluster; these messaging and component files are tightly integrated with the main viewer zone rather than standing independently.
- Static image assets (SourceVision-F.png, SourceVision.png) and build scripts (build.js, dev.js) co-cluster with runtime messaging infrastructure, indicating this zone is partly a catch-all for files without strong import-graph affinity.
- The messaging entry points (messaging/index.ts, request-dedup.ts) align with the CLAUDE.md-declared viewer-message-pipeline internal zone, confirming these files are genuine messaging infrastructure that the main viewer zone consumes through external.ts.
- Bidirectional imports with web-application-core (38 total) suggest the community detection threshold fragmented what is architecturally a single layered zone; consolidating messaging and peripheral components into the core zone may better reflect intent.
- Static binary assets (PNG files) and build scripts are co-clustered with runtime messaging modules; these artifacts have no import relationships and are included purely by package-level proximity, not semantic cohesion.
- MCP route handler (routes-mcp.ts) landing in this peripheral zone rather than the main server cluster may make it harder to audit the full MCP request lifecycle alongside the other server routes in web-application-core.
- Zone "web-viewer-messaging-pipeline" has files across 11 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- routes-mcp.ts is imported exclusively by packages/web/src/server/start.ts yet community detection placed it in this peripheral zone rather than the server cluster; the file's sole consumer is the server composition root, confirming it belongs with server routes not messaging infrastructure.
- routes-mcp.ts has exactly one consumer (server/start.ts) inside the server composition root but landed in the messaging-pipeline zone; this placement splits the MCP request lifecycle across two zones and obscures the full server routing surface during audits.
- performance/index.ts (viewer zone) re-exports the entire crash-detector public API from ../crash/index.js, acting as a secondary barrel for a module that has its own barrel (crash/index.ts). This dual-barrel pattern means crash-detector consumers may import from either path, making the crash zone's actual consumer count undercount in the import graph and obscuring the true dependency surface.
- performance/index.ts re-exports all of crash-detector's public symbols from ../crash/index.js, creating a second canonical path for the same module. Consumers that import crash symbols from performance/index.ts will not appear as direct crash-zone dependents in import graph analysis, making crash zone coupling invisible to tooling.
- Zone name 'messaging-pipeline' conflates three unrelated concerns under one label: (1) genuine messaging infrastructure (messaging/index.ts, request-dedup.ts), (2) peripheral UI components (guide.ts, status-filter.ts), and (3) build artifacts and static assets (build.js, dev.js, PNG files, package.json). The name accurately describes only the first group, creating false precision that misleads contributors about the zone's actual scope.
- web-viewer-messaging-pipeline is the only zone in this batch with both below-threshold cohesion (0.55) and above-threshold coupling (0.45) simultaneously — all other web zones have coupling ≤ 0.13. This joint fragility profile makes it the sole high-risk zone in this batch under the standard cohesion+coupling risk matrix.
- Zone name 'messaging-pipeline' is misleading: it groups server route handlers, build scripts, static image assets, peripheral UI components, and messaging infrastructure under a single messaging-specific label. Rename to 'web-peripheral' or 'web-seam' to reflect its catch-all reality, or split into a dedicated messaging zone and a build-artifacts zone to bring the name into alignment with actual content.
- This is the only web zone meeting the joint fragility criterion (cohesion < 0.6 AND coupling > 0.3). Its 10 entry points expose a wide API surface into bidirectionally-coupled neighbors. Treat refactoring changes in this zone as high-risk until cohesion improves or the catch-all content is redistributed.
- [call graph] 755 internal calls, 11 outgoing, 11 incoming (cohesion: 0.99, coupling: 0.01)

</insights>
